name: Nikki's Eggplant Parmesan
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the eggplant and trim ends. Cut into 1/4-inch thick rounds.
2. Place on a broiler pan or baking sheet; brush surface with oil.
3. Broil about 3 inches from heat for 5 minutes until lightly browned.
4. Turn, brush uncooked side with oil, and broil for another 3 minutes on the other side.
5. Arrange eggplant slices in a single layer at the bottom of a 2-quart baking dish (a deeper casserole yields a thicker, creamier result; a shallower dish gives more layers and a crisper top).
6. Cover with a single layer of mozzarella cheese, then spoon over enough tomato sauce to cover.
7. Sprinkle generously with grated Parmesan.
8. Repeat layers until all eggplant is used, adding the Parmesan layer every other time.
9. End with tomato sauce and additional Parmesan cheese. Any extra sauce can be poured over the top if needed for moisture; it will filter down during baking.
10. Refrigerate for several hours if desired (then let cool to room temperature before baking or add 5–10 minutes to bake time).
11. Bake in a 350°F oven for 15–20 minutes until the sauce bubbles and cheese melts.
12. Serve from the casserole using a large cooking spoon, cutting the cheese strands with scissors.
